sn12.68:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Musila, Ven. Paviṭṭha, Ven. Nārada, and Ven. Ānanda were staying near Kosambī at Ghosita’s monastery.
sn12.68:1.2: Then Ven. Paviṭṭha said to Ven. Musila,
sn12.68:1.3: “Musila, my friend, putting aside conviction, putting aside preference, putting aside tradition, putting aside reasoning through analogies, putting aside an agreement through pondering views: Do you have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:1.4: ‘From birth as a requisite condition come aging-&-death’?”
sn12.68:1.5: “Yes, Paviṭṭha my friend. Putting aside conviction… preference… tradition… reasoning through analogies… an agreement through pondering views, I do have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:1.6: ‘From birth as a requisite condition come aging-&-death.’”
sn12.68:2.2: ‘From becoming as a requisite condition comes birth’ .…
sn12.68:2.3: ‘From clinging/sustenance as a requisite condition comes becoming’.…
sn12.68:2.4: ‘From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging/sustenance’.…
sn12.68:2.5: ‘From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving’.…
sn12.68:2.6: ‘From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling’.…
sn12.68:2.7: ‘From the six sense media as a requisite condition comes contact’.…
sn12.68:2.8: ‘From name-&-form as a requisite condition come the six sense media’.…
sn12.68:2.9: ‘From consciousness as a requisite condition comes name-&-form’.…
sn12.68:2.10: ‘From fabrications as a requisite condition comes consciousness.’]
sn12.68:2.11: “Musila, my friend, putting aside conviction, putting aside preference, putting aside tradition, putting aside reasoning through analogies, putting aside an agreement through pondering views: Do you have truly personal knowledge that, ‘From ignorance as a requisite condition come fabrications’?”
sn12.68:2.12: “Yes, Paviṭṭha my friend. Putting aside conviction… preference… tradition… reasoning through analogies… an agreement through pondering views, I do have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:2.13: ‘From ignorance as a requisite condition come fabrications.’”
sn12.68:3.1: “Musila, my friend, putting aside conviction, putting aside preference, putting aside tradition, putting aside reasoning through analogies, putting aside an agreement through pondering views: Do you have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:3.2: ‘From the cessation of birth comes the cessation of aging-&-death’?”
sn12.68:3.3: “Yes, Paviṭṭha my friend. Putting aside conviction… preference… tradition… reasoning through analogies… an agreement through pondering views, I do have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:3.4: ‘From the cessation of birth comes the cessation of aging-&-death.’”
sn12.68:4.2: ‘From the cessation of becoming comes the cessation of birth’.…
sn12.68:4.3: ‘From the cessation of clinging/sustenance comes the cessation of becoming’.…
sn12.68:4.4: ‘From the cessation of craving comes the cessation of clinging/sustenance’.…
sn12.68:4.5: ‘From the cessation of feeling comes the cessation of craving’.…
sn12.68:4.6: ‘From the cessation of contact comes the cessation of feeling’.…
sn12.68:4.7: ‘From the cessation of the six sense media comes the cessation of contact’.…
sn12.68:4.8: ‘From the cessation of name-&-form comes the cessation of the six sense media’.…
sn12.68:4.9: ‘From the cessation of consciousness comes the cessation of name-&-form’.…
sn12.68:4.10: ‘From the cessation of fabrications comes the cessation of consciousness.’]
sn12.68:4.11: “Musila, my friend, putting aside conviction, putting aside preference, putting aside tradition, putting aside reasoning through analogies, putting aside an agreement through pondering views: Do you have truly personal knowledge that, ‘From the cessation of ignorance comes the cessation of fabrications’?”
sn12.68:4.12: “Yes, Paviṭṭha my friend. Putting aside conviction… preference… tradition… reasoning through analogies… an agreement through pondering views, I do have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:4.13: ‘From the cessation of ignorance comes the cessation of fabrications.’”
sn12.68:5.1: “Musila, my friend, putting aside conviction, putting aside preference, putting aside tradition, putting aside reasoning through analogies, putting aside an agreement through pondering views: Do you have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:5.3: “Yes, Paviṭṭha my friend. Putting aside conviction… preference… tradition… reasoning through analogies… an agreement through pondering views, I do have truly personal knowledge that,
sn12.68:6.1: “Then, Ven. Musila, you are an arahant whose effluents are ended.”
sn12.68:6.2: When this was said, Ven. Musila was silent.
sn12.68:7.2: “Paviṭṭha my friend, it would be good if I were to get that question.
sn12.68:7.4: and I will answer it for you.”
sn12.68:7.5: “Then Ven. Nārada will get that question.
sn12.68:7.6: I will ask Ven. Nārada that question,
sn12.68:7.7: and may he answer that question for me.”
sn12.68:8.1: [Ven. Paviṭṭha asks the same questions of Ven. Nārada, who gives the same answers as Ven. Musila.]
sn12.68:13.1: “Then, Ven. Nārada, you are an arahant whose effluents are ended.”
sn12.68:13.2: “My friend, although I have seen properly with right discernment, as it has come to be, that ‘The cessation of becoming is unbinding,’ still I am not an arahant whose effluents are ended.
sn12.68:13.3: It’s as if there were a well along a road in a desert, with neither rope nor water bucket.
sn12.68:13.4: A man would come along overcome by heat, oppressed by the heat, exhausted, dehydrated, & thirsty.
sn12.68:13.5: He would look into the well and would have knowledge of ‘water,’ but he would not dwell touching it with his body.
sn12.68:13.6: In the same way, although I have seen properly with right discernment, as it has come to be, that ‘The cessation of becoming is unbinding,’ still I am not an arahant whose effluents are ended.”
sn12.68:14.1: When this was said, Ven. Ānanda said to Ven. Paviṭṭha,
sn12.68:14.2: “When he speaks in this way, friend Paviṭṭha, what do you have to say about Ven. Nārada?”
sn12.68:14.3: “When Ven. Nārada speaks in this way, friend Ānanda, I have nothing to say about Ven. Nārada except that (he is) admirable & skillful.”
